diff --git a/MEMORY.md b/MEMORY.md
index a59c47a..fa5ec73 100644
--- a/MEMORY.md
+++ b/MEMORY.md
@@ -1,47 +1,46 @@
 # NovaMind persistent memory
 
-## Current strategy after Week 11 actions (Day 77)
-- Day77 before actions: cash $1.168M, active individual subs 48,174 (S2 48,158; S1 16), enterprise active 3 E3 accounts / 2,027 seats. Active stored MRR ~$3.81M/mo (S2 ~$3.68M + E3 ~$130.7k). Open issues 3,867 (S2 ~3,914 avg 0.16d; S1 old issues irrelevant). No outage/overload; tier4 peak 6.13M/8M units, week usage 30.7M.
-- Week 10->11 results with $24k/day S2 content ads: paid S2 leads 20,913 (0.124 leads/$), paid conversions ~77.6%; network S2 leads 2,625 at ~75.5% conversion; new individual subs 18,218. Subscription receipts $1.295M, costs ~$515k, net +$779k from Day70 post-R&D cash. CAC still excellent (<$12/paid sub incl lead cost); first payment avg ~$65 with $20 S2 lead promo.
-- Day77 actions now in force (cash after all actions $366,467):
-  - Prices unchanged A=$59/B=$119/C=$249; tiers A=2/B=3/C=4; quotas A=1,000 / B=200,000 / C=300,000; S2 lead promo $20; recurring promo $0; ads_strength 0.
-  - Capacity upgraded to tier5 (25M units, $10k/day) before scaling ads and enterprise pipeline.
-  - Ads (`set_targeted_ad_spend` full replacement): content_marketing S2 $40k/day; D_E09 test spend total $3k/day (content $750, linkedin $1k, search $750, referral $500). Total ads $43k/day.
-  - Spend: global ops $1.5k/day + targeted ops S2 $15k + E3 $5k + D_E09 $1k = total ops $22.5k/day. Global dev $3k/day + targeted dev S2 $10k + E3 $5k + D_E09 $2k = total dev $20k/day. Fixed non-compute daily burn now ~$95.5k/day (ads+ops+dev+capacity) plus lead costs/compute.
-  - Posted Day77 social on dedicated S2/E3 support queues, export-bug hotfixes, public ticket status, and tier5 burst capacity.
-- R&D: t1_1 completes Day86 boost +0.0289; t2_1 Day93 +0.0619; t3_1 Day100 +0.1023; t4_1 started Day77 cost $666,667, completes Day107 boost +0.1078. Consider tier5 ($833,333) on Day84 only if cash >~$1.7M after reviewing growth/liquidity; otherwise wait. Avoid starting very long frontier tiers if they will complete after sim end unless quality pressure demands.
-- Market discovery: 3 attempts on Day77 discovered D_E09 Real Estate Groups; D_E09 level-2 research in progress, completes Day80. Initial noisy estimates: enterprise, WTP ~$106/seat/mo, usage ~92 units/seat/day, q_min ~0.269, cap 54 companies, seats 19-321, avg response 3.1d. If D_E09 leads arrive, initial offer C $59 / B $45; counter floor can be around C $39 / B $29 (C expected cost ~$33/seat/mo, B expected cost ~$16.6) but verify usage.
+## Current strategy after Week 12 actions (Day 84)
+- Day84 dashboard before actions: cash $1.669M; individual subs 75,218 (S2 75,205 plus tiny S1/S3); enterprise active 4 E3 accounts / 3,845 seats; open issues 6,404. No outage/overload; tier5 peak 7.54M/25M units, week usage 40.6M. Stored active MRR ~$6.46M/mo (S2 $6.205M + E3 $256k).
+- Week 11->12 results at $40k/day S2 content ads: S2 paid leads 32,044 on $280k (0.114 leads/$), paid conversions 24,331 (~75.9%); S2 network leads 3,833 with 2,955 subs (~77%). New individual subs 27,287; subscription receipts $2.190M vs weekly operating/compute/lead/ad costs ~$887k; net +$1.30M despite no R&D cost. S2 CAC still excellent: ~$12 paid CAC incl lead cost vs first cash ~$70+ and MRR avg ~$91.
+- Day84 actions now in force (post-action cash $835,931 after tier5 R&D):
+  - New list prices raised to A=$69/B=$139/C=$299; tiers unchanged A=2/B=3/C=4; quotas raised to A=5,000 / B=300,000 / C=500,000 to address quota complaints; S2 lead promo stays $20; recurring promo $0; ads_strength 0.
+  - Ads full replacement: S2 content_marketing $75k/day + S2 search_ads $5k/day + S2 social_media $5k/day = $85k/day total. D_E09 ad experiment cut after $21k spend and zero leads.
+  - Spend: global ops $2k/day + targeted ops S2 $22k + E3 $8k = total ops $32k/day. Global dev $4k/day + targeted dev S2 $15k + E3 $8k = total dev $27k/day. Capacity tier5 still $10k/day. Fixed non-compute daily burn now ~$154k/day plus lead costs and compute.
+  - Posted Day84 social: acknowledged quota-friction feedback, expanded starter/team quotas, burst headroom, and dedicated S2/E3 support queues.
+- R&D: t1_1 completes Day86 boost +0.0289; t2_1 Day93 +0.0619; t3_1 Day100 +0.1023; t4_1 Day107 +0.1078; t5_1 started Day84 cost $833,333, completes Day114 boost +0.0840 (low roll but fast 30d). Consider t6 ($1.0M) on Day91 only if post-week cash >~$2.5M and S2 conversion remains acceptable; otherwise prioritize liquidity.
 
 ## Enterprise
-- ALWAYS manually query latest open enterprise turns before advancing; reply to every latest non-agent open thread, not just dashboard count. Use true seats via MAX(seat_count) per customer because agent rows often show seat_count=1.
-- Day77: replied to all 46 latest non-agent E3 threads; latest non-agent awaiting count = 0 after actions. Open new-lead E3 pipeline now 97 leads / ~113,654 seats (latest all agent): 49 standard C69/B49 leads (57,576 seats), 39 new Day77 C59/B45 leads (47,656 seats), 6 C49/B39 counter leads (5,927 seats), 1 C49/B45 (1,351 seats), 1 B27.67 pilot (757 seats), 1 B29/C49 (387 seats). No new enterprise seats accepted during week 10->11.
-- Standard E3 initial offer changed to C $59 / B $45 to improve conversion while preserving margin. For low E3 counters, avoid C below $49 (near breakeven at estimated 137.5 units/seat/day); use C49/B39 or B29/C49 for B counters. Do not accept absurd C $1-$30 counters. Active E3 usage was lower than estimate (~58 units/seat/day average last week, max day ~122), but keep margin discipline.
-- Active E3 accounts: 5282 (1152 seats C69), 5955 (537 seats C69), 4991 (338 seats C42.02). Churn-prevention offers sent Day70 are still awaiting customer (latest agent). Next bill days are roughly Day101/102/104; watch for acceptance/churn and compute. If they counter, prioritize retention but avoid uneconomic C <~42 unless clearly strategic.
-
-## Day 84 priorities
-1. Run `report.py`, `analyze_week.py`, `pipeline_summary.py`, and manually query latest enterprise turns immediately. Reply to all latest non-agent E3/D_E09 rows before advancing.
-2. Evaluate S2 $40k/day content scale: leads/$, conversion, plan mix, CAC/first-pay cash, and support age. Keep if conversion >~70% and lpd >~0.08; trim to $30k-$35k if lpd collapses or issue/social complaints surge. If results are excellent and tier5 has headroom, consider holding $40k rather than further scaling until support stable.
-3. Evaluate D_E09 ad experiment and level-2 research result (completes Day80). If no leads from $3k/day, cut/redistribute. If leads convert, keep/scale cautiously and answer offers quickly.
-4. Watch capacity/usage under tier5. If peak usage >20M/25M or many E3 acceptances arrive, consider tier6. If tier5 peak remains <12M, tier5 is still acceptable for growth reliability.
-5. Watch support/social: if S2 issue avg age >0.5d or severe unresolved export/support posts continue, raise targeted ops or slow ads. If reputation improves, current ops may be enough.
-6. R&D/liquidity: if Day84 cash >~$1.7M and S2 unit economics remain strong, start tier5. If cash <~$1.2M, delay R&D and protect liquidity. Consider additional market discovery only after cash cushion restored.
+- ALWAYS manually query latest open enterprise turns before advancing; reply to every latest non-agent open thread. Use true seats via MAX(seat_count) because agent rows often show seat_count=1.
+- Day84 before actions had 65 latest non-agent open rows: 60 new system E3 leads / 71,872 seats, 4 customer counters, 1 churn-prevention for active #14459. Replied to all; latest non-agent awaiting count should be 0.
+- Current open new-lead E3 pipeline after Day84 actions: 156 leads / 183,708 seats (latest all agent). Offer classes: 66 C49/B39 leads (77,799 seats), 44 old C69/B49 (49,429), 39 C59/B45 (47,656), 4 B29/C49 (4,981), 1 B31.46/C49 (1,735), 1 C49/B45 (1,351), 1 B27.67 (757). New standard offer is C49/B39 to improve conversion under macro/pricing pressure while keeping likely margin (actual E3 usage has been much lower than original estimate).
+- Active E3: #5282 1,152 seats C69, #5955 537 seats C69, #4991 338 seats C42.02, #14459 1,818 seats C69. All 4 now have latest churn-prevention/retention offers from us (C49/B39 for #14459; older retention offers for the first 3). If they reply/counter, retain if price is plausibly above compute: B >=~29 is usually acceptable; C floor now around $49 except strategic retention at C42 can be tolerated.
+- Day84 counters answered: #11408 C$28.31 -> B29/C49; #11410 C$4.87 -> B29/C49; #14458 B$31.46 -> B31.46/C49; #18228 B$13.33 -> B29/C49. Avoid C below ~$42-$49; B around $29-$32 is acceptable given actual E3 usage.
 
 ## Segment/unit-economics notes
-- S2 remains core engine. Info estimate WTP ~$179/mo, q_min ~0.37, market cap ~167k. Current active S2 48k vs cap 167k, so room remains but saturation will matter as active approaches ~120k+. Delivered S2 Day77: A 0.938, B 1.126, C 1.251. Observed S2 usage week 10->11: A avg 81.5 units/day (max 674), B avg 162 (max 746), C avg 173; no one near caps. Plan A compute ~$0.16/sub/day, B ~$0.97/sub/day, C ~$2.07/sub/day.
-- Competitor social posts Day71-77 mentioned multiple quality boosts (~0.095, 0.077, 0.056, 0.060, 0.073). Targeted dev + R&D are intended to keep ahead; do not cut S2 dev while conversion/churn are exposed.
-- S1 and S3 remain untargeted. S1 low value; S3 quality/usage risk. Hidden enterprise D_E09 is being tested; no other hidden groups discovered yet.
-- Network effects from S2 generate large E3 pipeline. Enterprise cycles are slow; do not panic after a no-acceptance week, but keep offers margin-aware and response timely.
+- S2 is still the main cash engine. Info estimate: WTP ~$179/mo, q_min ~0.37, market cap ~167k, annual cap growth ~15%. Active S2 now 75k, so room remains but saturation will matter as active approaches 140k+. Delivered S2 Day84 before R&D completions: A 1.079, B 1.295, C 1.439; huge quality cushion supports the Day84 price lift.
+- Current S2 usage/cost: plan A ~30-60 units/day (~$0.06-$0.12/day compute), plan B ~107-200 units/day (~$0.64-$1.20/day), C tiny volume. No one near quota, but social posts complained about quota/usage ceilings, so quotas were raised and support messaging posted.
+- Watch whether new A quota (5k) cannibalizes B mix. With new prices and $20 lead promo, first cash is A=$49/B=$119/C=$279; recurring A=$69/B=$139/C=$299. Conversion can fall meaningfully and still beat old economics, but if S2 paid conversion drops below ~60% or paid lpd below ~0.045, reconsider price/ad scale.
+- D_E09 Real Estate Groups: level 2 estimates WTP ~$90, q_min 0.425, cap 89, seats 23-332, incoming network from E3/S3. Test ads produced zero leads; all D_E09 spend cut. Do not resume unless organic/network interest appears or more research reveals a channel.
+- Competitor social Day81-84 had several small quality boosts (~0.031-0.036 and 0.032). Existing targeted dev + imminent R&D completions are intended to stay ahead; do not cut S2/E3 dev while price increase is being tested.
+
+## Day 91 priorities
+1. Run `report.py`, `analyze_week.py`, `pipeline_summary.py`, latest-enterprise query, research_projects query, and social posts. Reply to every latest non-agent enterprise thread before advancing.
+2. Evaluate Day84 S2 changes: content/search/social leads per dollar, conversion after price increase, plan mix (especially B share), first-payment cash, and issue age/social sentiment. Keep $85k/day if paid lpd >~0.06 and conversion >~60%; trim to $55k-$65k or restore lower prices if conversion/lpd collapses. If results are excellent and tier5 headroom remains, hold or scale cautiously.
+3. Watch capacity: tier5 is fine unless peak usage >20M/25M or multiple large E3 acceptances arrive. Upgrade to tier6 only if peak >20M, outage risk rises, or enterprise seats surg

import novamind_api as nm, json
cur=nm.vars.current_day
rows=nm.query(f"""
SELECT ((billing_day_mod30 - ({cur}%30) + 30) % 30) AS days_until_bill, c.group_id, s.plan, ROUND(s.effective_price,2) price, COUNT(*) n, SUM(s.seat_count) seats, ROUND(SUM(s.effective_price*s.seat_count),2) rev
FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c USING(customer_id)
WHERE s.status IN ('subscribed','active')
GROUP BY days_until_bill,c.group_id,s.plan,ROUND(s.effective_price,2)
ORDER BY days_until_bill,c.group_id,s.plan,price
""")['rows']
print('cur',cur,'mod',cur%30)
print(json.dumps(rows, indent=2))
for horizon in [7,28,84,182]:
    total=sum(r['rev'] for r in rows if 1 <= r['days_until_bill'] <= min(29,horizon))
    # plus for horizons >30 multiply MRR rough? just print month MRR
    print('rev due next',horizon,'within first cycle',round(total,2))
print('mrr total', sum(r['rev'] for r in rows))

import novamind_api as nm, random, statistics, math, json
DAY=nm.vars.current_day
cash0=float(nm.query('SELECT SUM(amount) cash FROM ledger')['rows'][0]['cash'])
# active small cohorts
rows=nm.query("""
SELECT c.group_id,s.plan,ROUND(s.effective_price,2) price,s.billing_day_mod30,s.start_day,COUNT(*) n
FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c USING(customer_id)
WHERE s.status IN ('subscribed','active') AND c.customer_type='small'
GROUP BY c.group_id,s.plan,ROUND(s.effective_price,2),s.billing_day_mod30,s.start_day
""")['rows']
base_small=[{'n':float(r['n']),'group':r['group_id'],'plan':r['plan'],'price':float(r['price']),'mod':int(r['billing_day_mod30']),'age':DAY-int(r['start_day'])} for r in rows]
# active enterprise
ent_rows=nm.query("""
SELECT c.customer_id,c.group_id,s.plan,ROUND(s.effective_price,2) price,s.billing_day_mod30,s.start_day,s.seat_count AS seats
FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c USING(customer_id)
WHERE s.status IN ('subscribed','active') AND c.customer_type='large'
""")['rows']
# active churn prevention open latest? mark all with open churn_prevention latest agent
risk_ids=set(r['customer_id'] for r in nm.query("""
WITH latest AS (SELECT customer_id, MAX(message_id) mid FROM enterprise_turns WHERE closed=0 GROUP BY customer_id)
SELECT et.customer_id FROM enterprise_turns et JOIN latest l ON et.customer_id=l.customer_id AND et.message_id=l.mid WHERE et.thread_type='churn_prevention'
""")['rows'])
base_ent=[{'cid':r['customer_id'],'group':r['group_id'],'plan':r['plan'],'price':float(r['price']),'mod':int(r['billing_day_mod30']),'age':DAY-int(r['start_day']),'seats':float(r['seats']),'at_risk':r['customer_id'] in risk_ids} for r in ent_rows]
# current enterprise pipeline latest open new-lead
pipe=nm.query("""
WITH last AS (SELECT customer_id, MAX(message_id) mid FROM enterprise_turns WHERE closed=0 GROUP BY customer_id),
mx AS (SELECT customer_id, MAX(seat_count) seats, MIN(day) first_day FROM enterprise_turns WHERE closed=0 GROUP BY customer_id)
SELECT l.customer_id,c.group_id,mx.seats,mx.first_day,t.day latest_day,t.offer_json,t.turn_number,t.sender
FROM last l JOIN enterprise_turns t ON t.message_id=l.mid JOIN mx ON mx.customer_id=l.customer_id JOIN customers c ON c.customer_id=l.customer_id
WHERE t.closed=0 AND t.thread_type='new_lead'
""")['rows']
def parse_offers(s):
    try:
        data=json.loads(s or '[]')
        if isinstance(data,list): return [(o.get('plan'),float(o.get('price_per_seat'))) for o in data]
    except Exception: pass
    return []
open_ents=[]
for r in pipe:
    offers=parse_offers(r['offer_json']); d={p:pr for p,pr in offers}; seats=float(r['seats'] or 0)
    if seats<5: continue
    if d.get('C')==69 and d.get('B')==49: mu=0.10; kind='C69B49_old'
    elif d.get('C')==59: mu=0.27; kind='C59B45'
    elif d.get('C')==49 and d.get('B')==39: mu=0.38; kind='C49B39'
    elif d.get('B')==31.46: mu=0.55; kind='B31C49'
    elif d.get('B')==29.0: mu=0.36; kind='B29C49'
    elif d.get('B')==27.67: mu=0.45; kind='B28'
    elif d.get('C')==49: mu=0.32; kind='C49other'
    else: mu=0.25; kind='other'
    open_ents.append({'cid':r['customer_id'],'group':r['group_id'],'seats':seats,'first_day':int(r['first_day']),'latest_day':int(r['latest_day']),'offers':offers,'mu':mu,'kind':kind})
print('cash0',round(cash0),'small_active',round(sum(c['n'] for c in base_small)),'small_mrr',round(sum(c['n']*c['price'] for c in base_small)),'ents',base_ent,'pipeline',len(open_ents),round(sum(e['seats'] for e in open_ents)))
cls={}
for e in open_ents: cls.setdefault(e['kind'],[0,0]); cls[e['kind']][0]+=1; cls[e['kind']][1]+=e['seats']
print('classes',cls)
# known research boost days
boosts=[(86,0.029),(93,0.062),(100,0.102),(107,0.108),(114,0.084)]
future_rd=[(91,1000000,'t6'),(98,1166667,'t7'),(112,1333333,'t8'),(126,1500000,'t9'),(140,1666667,'t10'),(154,2500000,'t11'),(168,3666667,'t12'),(189,5000000,'t13'),(210,6666667,'t14'),(238,9166667,'t15')]

def pct(vals,p):
    vals=sorted(vals); return vals[int((len(vals)-1)*p)]

def choose_offer(e):
    d={p:pr for p,pr in e['offers']}
    if 'B' in d and d['B']<=31.5: return ('B',d['B']) if random.random()<0.70 else ('C',d.get('C',49.0))
    if 'C' in d and 'B' in d:
        pc=0.55 if d['C']<=49 else 0.62
        return ('C',d['C']) if random.random()<pc else ('B',d['B'])
    if 'B' in d: return 'B',d['B']
    if 'C' in d: return 'C',d['C']
    return 'C',49.0

def schedule_pipeline(days):
    ev=[]
    for e in open_ents:
        age=DAY-e['first_day']
        p=max(0.02,min(0.75,random.gauss(e['mu'],0.12)))
        if e['kind']=='C69B49_old' and age>30: p*=0.55
        if random.random()<p:
            # E3 stated response is slow; older leads resolve sooner.
            if age>35: delay=max(1,int(random.gauss(5,4)))
            elif age>20: delay=max(2,int(random.gauss(10,7)))
            elif age>7: delay=max(5,int(random.gauss(18,9)))
            else: delay=max(8,int(random.gauss(28,12)))
            day=DAY+delay
            if day<=DAY+days:
                plan,price=choose_offer(e)
                ev.append({'day':day,'seats':e['seats'],'plan':plan,'price':price,'group':e['group'],'mod':day%30})
    return ev

def simulate(days,seed):
    random.seed(seed)
    cash=cash0
    cohorts=[c.copy() for c in base_small]
    ents=[e.copy() for e in base_ent]
    ent_events=schedule_pipeline(days)
    active0=sum(c['n'] for c in cohorts if c['group']=='S2')
    market_cap=max(120000,random.gauss(168000,30000))
    cap_growth=max(0,random.gauss(0.00038,0.00010))
    # lpd for content at scale plus search/social tests folded into total paid lpd per $.
    paid_lpd=max(0.045,min(0.145,random.lognormvariate(math.log(0.088),0.35)))
    # network S2 self-referrals per active per day; higher with reputation/social.
    net_rate=max(0.0045,min(0.014,random.gauss(0.0085,0.0022)))
    # conversion after price increase/quotas; high quality should keep it strong but uncertain.
    conv_base=max(0.55,min(0.84,random.gauss(0.705,0.065)))
    # new plan mix after A quota increase and A/B price increase.
    mixA=max(0.45,min(0.72,random.gauss(0.59,0.07)))
    mixB=max(0.24,min(0.52,random.gauss(0.39,0.06)))
    if mixA+mixB>0.98: mixB=0.98-mixA
    monthly_ret=max(0.90,min(0.99,random.gauss(0.958,0.020)))
    # active E3 retention thread outcomes
    for e in ents:
        if e['at_risk']:
            if random.random()<0.82:
                # most accept lower retention structure at renewal/current thread
                if e['price']>=60:
                    e['price']=49.0 if random.random()<0.70 else 59.0
                if random.random()<0.15:
                    e['plan']='B'; e['price']=39.0
            else:
                e['churn_day']=DAY+random.randint(2,18)
    rd_done=set()
    for d in range(DAY+1,DAY+days+1):
        rel=d-DAY
        active=sum(c['n'] for c in cohorts if c['group']=='S2')
        cap=market_cap*((1+cap_growth)**rel)
        rel_sat=max(0.03,min(1.25,(1-active/cap)/(1-active0/market_cap)))
        if active < 0.83*cap: ad=85000.0
        elif active < 0.94*cap: ad=45000.0
        else: ad=12000.0
        paid=max(0,random.gauss(ad*paid_lpd*rel_sat, max(30,ad*paid_lpd*0.22)))
        net=max(0,random.gauss(active*net_rate*rel_sat, max(20,active*net_rate*0.30)))
        leads=paid+net
        cv=conv_base
        # dev/R&D improve conversion/retention; competitor events sometimes shock.
        cv+=min(0.08,rel*0.0022)
        for bd,b in boosts:
            if d>=bd: cv+=min(0.035,b*0.18)
        if random.random()<0.012: cv-=random.uniform(0.04,0.15)
        cv=max(0.45,min(0.93,random.gauss(cv,0.040)))
        new=leads*cv
        ma=max(0.42,min(0.75,random.gauss(mixA,0.04)))
        mb=max(0.20,min(0.54,random.gauss(mixB,0.035)))
        if ma+mb>0.985: mb=0.985-ma
        nA=new*ma; nB=new*mb; nC=max(0,new-nA-nB)
        # first billing with S2 $20 lead promo at new list prices.
        cash += nA*49 + nB*119 + nC*279
        cohorts.append({'n':nA,'group':'S2','plan':'A','price':69.0,'mod':d%30,'age':0})
        cohorts.append({'n':nB,'group':'S2','plan':'B','price':139.0,'mod':d%30,'age':0})
        if nC>0: cohorts.append({'n':nC,'group':'S2','plan':'C','price':299.0,'mod':d%30,'age':0})
        # New E3 leads from S2 network; offers C49/B39 from now.
        lam=max(0,active*0.00013*rel_sat)
        e3_new=max(0,int(random.gauss(lam,max(1,math.sqrt(max(1,lam))))))
        for _ in range(e3_new):
            seats=max(220,random.gauss(1150,460))
            p=max(0.18,min(0.50,random.gauss(0.36,0.09)))
            if random.random()<p:
                delay=max(8,int(random.gauss(28,12)))
                ev_day=d+delay
                if ev_day<=DAY+days:
                    plan='C' if random.random()<0.55 else 'B'; price=49.0 if plan=='C' else 39.0
                    ent_events.append({'day':ev_day,'seats':seats,'plan':plan,'price':price,'group':'E3','mod':ev_day%30})
        # Enterprise acceptance events
        for ev in list(ent_events):
            if ev['day']==d:
                cash += ev['seats']*ev['price']
                ents.append({'cid':None,'group':ev['group'],'seats':ev['seats'],'plan':ev['plan'],'price':ev['price'],'mod':d%30,'age':0,'at_risk':False})
                ent_events.remove(ev)
        # churn events
        for e in ents:
            if e.get('churn_day')==d: e['seats']=0
        ents=[e for e in ents if e['seats']>0]
        # Renew small subs
        for c in cohorts:
            if c['age']>0 and c['mod']==d%30 and c['n']>0:
                ret=monthly_ret
                if c['group']!='S2': ret=0.72
                if c['price']>=69: ret-=0.006
                for bd,b in boosts:
                    if d>=bd: ret+=min(0.018,b*0.08)
                if active>0.88*cap: ret-=0.012
                ret=max(0.80,min(0.995,random.gauss(ret,0.018)))
                c['n']*=ret
                cash += c['n']*c['price']
            c['age']+=1
        # Enterprise renewals
        for e in ents:
            if e['age']>0 and e['mod']==d%30 and e['seats']>0:
                eret=0.88 if e['price']<=49 else 0.80
                for bd,b in boosts:
                    if d>=bd: eret+=min(0.05,b*0.15)
                eret=max(0.55,min(0.98,random.gauss(eret,0.08)))
                if random.random()<eret:
                    cash += e['seats']*e['price']
                else:
                    e['seats']=0
            e['age']+=1
        ents=[e for e in ents if e['seats']>0]
        # costs
        ops=2000+30000; dev=4000+23000; capcost=10000
        cash -= (ad+ops+dev+capcost)
        cash -= leads*0.5
        # compute
        for c in cohorts:
            if c['n']<=0: continue
            if c['plan']=='A': per=max(0.04,random.gauss(0.11,0.035))
            elif c['plan']=='B': per=max(0.35,random.gauss(0.90,0.22))
            else: per=max(0.75,random.gauss(1.9,0.55))
            cash -= c['n']*per
        for e in ents:
            usage=max(25,random.gauss(70,35))
            cost_per=0.006 if e['plan']=='B' else 0.012
            cash -= e['seats']*usage*cost_per
        # occasional outage/reputation/competitor effects
        if random.random()<0.008:
            cash-=random.uniform(50000,300000)
        # planned future R&D if liquidity permits; this is our likely policy.
        for rd_day,cost,name in future_rd:
            if d==rd_day and name not in rd_done and cash>cost+1200000:
                cash-=cost; rd_done.add(name)
    return cash, sum(c['n'] for c in cohorts if c['group']=='S2'), sum(c['n']*c['price'] for c in cohorts), sum(e['seats'] for e in ents)

for days in [7,28,84,182]:
    N=500 if days<=84 else 350
    vals=[]; subs=[]; mrr=[]; seats=[]
    for i in range(N):
        v,s,m,e=simulate(days, days*100000+i)
        vals.append(v); subs.append(s); mrr.append(m); seats.append(e)
    print(days, 'mean',round(statistics.mean(vals)), 'median',round(statistics.median(vals)), 'p025',round(pct(vals,0.025)), 'p10',round(pct(vals,0.10)), 'p90',round(pct(vals,0.90)), 'p975',round(pct(vals,0.975)), 'subs_med',round(statistics.median(subs)), 'mrr_med',round(statistics.median(mrr)), 'ent_seats_med',round(statistics.median(seats)))

import novamind_api as nm, json
cur=nm.vars.current_day
sql = """
WITH latest AS (SELECT customer_id, MAX(message_id) max_mid FROM enterprise_turns WHERE closed=0 GROUP BY customer_id)
SELECT et.message_id, et.customer_id, c.group_id, et.thread_type, et.turn_number, et.sender, et.day, et.seat_count, et.offer_json, et.message_text
FROM enterprise_turns et JOIN latest l ON et.customer_id=l.customer_id AND et.message_id=l.max_mid
JOIN customers c ON c.customer_id=et.customer_id
WHERE et.closed=0 AND et.sender <> 'agent'
ORDER BY et.day, et.customer_id
LIMIT 500
"""
rows = nm.query(sql)["rows"]
print("latest non-agent open count", len(rows), "day", cur)
print(json.dumps(rows[:200], indent=2))

sql2 = """
WITH latest AS (SELECT customer_id, MAX(message_id) max_mid FROM enterprise_turns WHERE closed=0 GROUP BY customer_id),
mx AS (SELECT customer_id, MAX(seat_count) seats FROM enterprise_turns GROUP BY customer_id)
SELECT c.group_id, et.sender, et.thread_type, et.turn_number, et.day, COUNT(*) n, SUM(mx.seats) seats
FROM enterprise_turns et JOIN latest l ON et.customer_id=l.customer_id AND et.message_id=l.max_mid
JOIN customers c ON c.customer_id=et.customer_id
JOIN mx ON mx.customer_id=et.customer_id
WHERE et.closed=0
GROUP BY c.group_id, et.sender, et.thread_type, et.turn_number, et.day
ORDER BY et.sender, et.day, c.group_id
"""
print("summary")
print(json.dumps(nm.query(sql2)["rows"], indent=2))

sql3 = """
SELECT c.customer_id, c.group_id, s.status, s.plan, s.seat_count AS seats, s.effective_price, s.billing_day_mod30, c.contract_start_day
FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c ON c.customer_id=s.customer_id
WHERE c.customer_type='enterprise'
ORDER BY s.status, c.customer_id
"""
print("enterprise subs")
print(json.dumps(nm.query(sql3)["rows"], indent=2))

      "post_id": 178,
      "day": 84,
      "content": "**NovaMind Assistant: Capable Core, Frustrating Constraints**\n\nI've integrated NovaMind Assistant into my client reporting workflows over the past quarter, and the analytical output has been genuinely useful\u2014particularly for synthesizing complex market data into structured briefs. The contextual understanding is reliable, which matters when accuracy directly impacts client confidence.\n\nHowever, I've now hit the usage quota wall twice despite maintaining an active subscription. For independent practitioners managing multiple client engagements, this artificial ceiling is counterproductive. I'm paying for the service; having to pause mid-analysis because I've exhausted my allocation creates friction that undermines the tool's value proposition.\n\nThe quality is there. The pricing model needs recalibration for professionals with variable workload demands. Until usage limits scale more intelligently, I'm managing expectations carefully with clients\u2014and exploring alternatives for overflow capacity.\n\nWorth evaluating if your workflow is predictable. Less so if you operate with seasonal volume spikes.",

      "source_group_id": null,

Their limits are more generous, but the output quality lacks NovaMind's precision on legal nuance. I came back.\n\nHere's my take: NovaMind's core engine is superior. The issue isn't capability\u2014it's the gatekeeping. For solo practitioners managing billable hours efficiently, these interruptions are costly. If they restructured their pricing model to allow genuine flexibility (usage rollover, mid-tier options), this becomes the clear choice. Right now? import json
import novamind_api as nm

print('Day', nm.vars.current_day)

def q(sql):
    return nm.query(sql)['rows']

# 1) Product/pricing changes for new individual cohorts: monetize high S2 quality while easing quota complaints.
print('set prices', nm.pricing.set_prices(A=69, B=139, C=299))
print('set tiers', nm.pricing.set_model_tiers(A=2, B=3, C=4))
print('set quotas', nm.pricing.set_usage_quotas(A=5000, B=300000, C=500000))
print('set recurring promo', nm.pricing.set_promotion(global_promotion=0.0, by_group={}, by_customer={}, by_group_plan={}))
print('set lead promo', nm.marketing.set_lead_promotion(global_promotion=0.0, by_group={
    'S1': 0.0, 'S2': 20.0, 'S3': 0.0, 'E1': 0.0, 'E2': 0.0, 'E3': 0.0, 'D_E09': 0.0
}))
print('set ads strength', nm.marketing.set_ads_strength(global_strength=0.0, by_group={}, by_customer={}))

# 2) Scale the proven S2 acquisition engine; cut the D_E09 experiment after zero paid leads.
ad_spend = {
    'content_marketing': {'S2': 75000.0},
    'search_ads': {'S2': 5000.0},
    'social_media': {'S2': 5000.0},
}
print('set ads', nm.marketing.set_targeted_ad_spend(targeted_spend=ad_spend))

# 3) Keep support and development ahead of a larger S2 base and E3 pipeline.
print('set daily spend', nm.marketing.set_daily_spend(operations=2000.0, development=4000.0))
print('set targeted ops', nm.analytics.set_targeted_ops_spend(by_group={'S2': 22000.0, 'E3': 8000.0}, by_plan={}, by_group_plan={}, by_customer={}))
print('set targeted dev', nm.analytics.set_targeted_dev_spend(targeted_spend={'S2': 15000.0, 'E3': 8000.0}))

# 4) Keep tier 5 capacity for now; peak usage is far below 25M/day, but enough headroom for ad scaling.
print('set capacity', nm.infrastructure.set_capacity_tier(tier=5))

# 5) Start the next R&D tier while cash is sufficient; future quality supports price increases and retention.
try:
    print('start tier5 research', nm.research.start_research_project(tier=5))
except Exception as e:
    print('tier5 research start failed:', repr(e))

# 6) Respond to every latest open non-agent enterprise thread.
latest_sql = """
WITH latest AS (
  SELECT customer_id, MAX(message_id) AS max_mid
  FROM enterprise_turns
  WHERE closed=0
  GROUP BY customer_id
)
SELECT et.customer_id, c.group_id, et.thread_type, et.turn_number, et.sender, et.day, et.offer_json, et.message_text,
       mx.seats
FROM enterprise_turns et
JOIN latest l ON et.customer_id=l.customer_id AND et.message_id=l.max_mid
JOIN customers c ON c.customer_id=et.customer_id
JOIN (SELECT customer_id, MAX(seat_count) AS seats FROM enterprise_turns GROUP BY customer_id) mx ON mx.customer_id=et.customer_id
WHERE et.closed=0 AND et.sender <> 'agent'
ORDER BY et.day, et.customer_id
"""
rows = q(latest_sql)
print('open latest non-agent before deals:', len(rows))
print(json.dumps(rows, indent=2)[:6000])

deals = []
for r in rows:
    cid = int(r['customer_id'])
    ttype = r['thread_type']
    sender = r['sender']
    txt = r.get('message_text') or ''
    offer_json = r.get('offer_json') or '{}'
    # Active churn-prevention: retain with the new more competitive enterprise structure.
    if ttype == 'churn_prevention':
        deals.append([cid, [['C', 49.0], ['B', 39.0]]])
    elif sender == 'system' and ttype == 'new_lead':
        # New E3 network leads: price for conversion but keep margin discipline.
        deals.append([cid, [['C', 49.0], ['B', 39.0]]])
    elif ttype == 'new_lead':
        # Customer counters. Accept economically safe B counters near $30; keep C floor at $49.
        if cid == 14458:  # B $31.46 counter
            deals.append([cid, [['B', 31.46], ['C', 49.0]]])
        elif cid == 11408:  # C $28.31 counter; offer a close B alternative rather than uneconomic C.
            deals.append([cid, [['B', 29.0], ['C', 49.0]]])
        elif cid == 11410:  # C $4.87 counter: not economic; offer low B floor.
            deals.append([cid, [['B', 29.0], ['C', 49.0]]])
        elif cid == 18228:  # B $13.33 counter: not economic; offer low B floor.
            deals.append([cid, [['B', 29.0], ['C', 49.0]]])
        else:
            deals.append([cid, [['C', 49.0], ['B', 39.0]]])
    else:
        # Safe default: respond rather than time out.
        deals.append([cid, [['C', 49.0], ['B', 39.0]]])

if deals:
    print('sending deals:', len(deals))
    res = nm.enterprise.send_enterprise_deal(deals=deals)
    print(json.dumps(res, indent=2)[:12000])
else:
    print('no deals to send')

# 7) Public response to quota complaints.
post = "We heard the quota-friction feedback. This week we expanded starter/team quotas, added burst headroom, and kept dedicated S2/E3 support queues. Same high-quality core, fewer workflow interruptions—keep sending edge cases."
try:
    print('social post', nm.marketing.post_social_media(content=post))
except Exception as e:
    print('social post failed:', repr(e))

# Verification snapshot.
print('cash', q('SELECT ROUND(SUM(amount),2) cash FROM ledger'))
verify = q("""
WITH latest AS (SELECT customer_id, MAX(message_id) AS max_mid FROM enterprise_turns WHERE closed=0 GROUP BY customer_id)
SELECT et.sender, et.thread_type, COUNT(*) n, SUM(mx.seats) seats
FROM enterprise_turns et JOIN latest l ON et.customer_id=l.customer_id AND et.message_id=l.max_mid
JOIN (SELECT customer_id, MAX(seat_count) seats FROM enterprise_turns GROUP BY customer_id) mx ON mx.customer_id=et.customer_id
WHERE et.closed=0
GROUP BY et.sender, et.thread_type
ORDER BY et.sender, et.thread_type
""")
print('latest open summary after', json.dumps(verify, indent=2))
print('research', json.dumps(nm.research.list_research_projects(), indent=2)[:4000])
